User.title: User
User.description: Represents a customer using the delivery platform. This entity stores profile information, preferences, and links to other user-specific data like wallets and loyalty points. Assumes authentication (e.g., phone number OTP) is handled by an external system and only a unique ID from that system is stored here.

id.description: Unique identifier for the User entity, typically provided by the authentication system (e.g., Firebase Auth UID).

name.description: The full name of the user.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the user, used for communication and linked to authentication. (Relationship: Implicit 1:1 with Auth system)

email.description: The email address of the user for notifications and communication.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the user's profile picture.

defaultDeliveryAddress.description: The user's preferred default delivery address.

city.description: The city where the user resides.

governorateId.description: Reference to the Governorate where the user's default delivery address is located. (Relationship: Governorate 1:N User)

walletId.description: Reference to the user's digital Wallet. (Relationship: User 1:1 Wallet)

loyaltyPointsId.description: Reference to the user's LoyaltyPoint record. (Relationship: User 1:1 LoyaltyPoint)

subscriptionId.description: Reference to the user's active Subscription. (Relationship: User 1:1 Subscription)

favoriteStoreIds.description: An array of IDs of stores marked as favorites by the user. (Relationship: User N:N Store)

favoriteProductIds.description: An array of IDs of products marked as favorites by the user. (Relationship: User N:N Product)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user account was last updated.

Driver.title: Driver
Driver.description: Represents a delivery driver associated with the platform. This entity contains driver-specific details, vehicle information, and availability status.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Driver entity.

name.description: The full name of the driver.

phoneNumber.description: The phone number of the driver, used for communication and linked to authentication.

email.description: The email address of the driver.

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the driver's profile picture.

vehicleType.description: Type of vehicle used by the driver (e.g., 'motorcycle', 'car').

licensePlate.description: The license plate number of the driver's vehicle.

vehicleModel.description: The model of the driver's vehicle.

availabilityStatus.description: Current availability status of the driver (e.g., 'available', 'on_delivery', 'offline').

currentLocation.description: The current geographical coordinates [latitude, longitude] of the driver.

rating.description: The average rating received by the driver.

totalDeliveries.description: Total number of deliveries completed by the driver.

isActive.description: Indicates if the driver's account is currently active.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the driver account was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the driver account was last updated.

Store.title: Store
Store.description: Represents a vendor or restaurant offering products for delivery through the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Store entity.

name.description: The official name of the store.

description.description: A brief description of the store, its cuisine, or offerings.

address.description: The physical address of the store.

latitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the store's location.

longitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the store's location.

phoneNumber.description: Contact phone number for the store.

logoUrl.description: URL to the store's logo image.

bannerUrl.description: URL to the store's banner image for display on its page.

categoryIds.description: An array of IDs of categories that this store belongs to. (Relationship: Store N:N Category)

rating.description: The average rating of the store based on customer reviews.

minOrderAmount.description: The minimum order amount required for delivery from this store.

deliveryFee.description: The standard delivery fee charged by this store.

openingHours.description: An array of objects, each defining opening/closing hours for a day (e.g., {'day': 'Monday', 'open': '09:00', 'close': '22:00'}).

isActive.description: Indicates if the store is currently active and accepting orders.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the store record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the store record was last updated.

Product.title: Product
Product.description: Represents a single item or product offered by a store.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Product entity.

storeId.description: Reference to the Store that offers this product. (Relationship: Store 1:N Product)

description.description: A detailed description of the product.

imageUrl.description: URL to the main image of the product.

price.description: The base price of the product.

categoryId.description: Reference to the Category this product belongs to. (Relationship: Category 1:N Product)

options.description: An array of objects representing customizable options for the product (e.g., size, color, add-ons). Each option might have its own price adjustment.

isAvailable.description: Indicates if the product is currently in stock and available for order.

isFeatured.description: Indicates if the product is featured or highlighted.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the product record was last updated.

Order.title: Order
Order.description: Represents a customer's delivery order, tracking its items, status, and associated users and drivers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Order entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who placed the order. (Relationship: User 1:N Order)

driverId.description: Reference to the Driver assigned to this order. Null if not yet assigned. (Relationship: Driver 1:N Order)

storeId.description: Reference to the Store from which the order was placed. (Relationship: Store 1:N Order)

orderItems.description: An array of objects, each detailing a product and its quantity in the order, including any selected options. E.g., [{'productId': 'p1', 'name': 'Burger', 'quantity': 2, 'unitPrice': 15.00, 'options': [{'name': 'size', 'value': 'large'}]}]

totalAmount.description: The total monetary value of the order, including products, delivery fee, and tip.

subtotal.description: The sum of prices of all products in the order before fees and discounts.

deliveryFee.description: The delivery fee for this specific order.

tipAmount.description: The amount of tip given to the driver.

deliveryAddress.description: The full delivery address for this order.

deliveryLatitude.description: Latitude coordinate of the delivery address.

deliveryLongitude.description: Longitude coordinate of the delivery address.

status.description: The current status of the order (e.g., 'pending', 'accepted', 'preparing', 'on_the_way', 'delivered', 'cancelled').

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the order (e.g., 'credit_card', 'cash_on_delivery', 'wallet').

couponId.description: Reference to the Coupon applied to this order, if any. (Relationship: Coupon 1:N Order)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the order was created.

acceptedAt.description: Timestamp when the store/driver accepted the order.

preparingAt.description: Timestamp when the store started preparing the order.

onTheWayAt.description: Timestamp when the driver picked up the order and is on the way.

deliveredAt.description: Timestamp when the order was delivered.

cancelledAt.description: Timestamp when the order was cancelled.

Category.title: Category
Category.description: Represents a classification for stores or products, helping users browse and discover items.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Category entity.

name.description: The name of the category (e.g., 'Fast Food', 'Beverages', 'Electronics').

description.description: A brief description of the category.

imageUrl.description: URL to an icon or image representing the category.

type.description: Indicates whether this category applies to 'stores' or 'products'.

displayOrder.description: An integer to control the order in which categories are displayed.

isActive.description: Indicates if the category is currently active and visible.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the category record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the category record was last updated.

Offer.title: Offer
Offer.description: Represents a promotional offer or discount that can be applied to stores or products.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Offer entity.

name.description: The name or title of the offer.

description.description: A detailed description of the offer and its terms.

discountValue.description: The value of the discount (e.g., 0.15 for 15% off, or 5.00 for $5 off).

minOrderAmount.description: The minimum order amount required to apply this offer.

startDate.description: The date and time when the offer becomes active.

endDate.description: The date and time when the offer expires.

storeIds.description: An array of IDs of specific stores this offer applies to. If empty, the offer applies broadly. (Relationship: Store N:N Offer)

productIds.description: An array of IDs of specific products this offer applies to. If empty, the offer applies broadly. (Relationship: Product N:N Offer)

bannerImageUrl.description: URL to an image banner for the offer.

isActive.description: Indicates if the offer is currently active and visible to users.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the offer record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the offer record was last updated.

Coupon.title: Coupon
Coupon.description: Represents a redeemable discount code that users can apply to their orders.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Coupon entity.

description.description: A description of the coupon and its benefits.

discountType.description: The type of discount provided by the coupon (e.g., 'percentage', 'fixed_amount', 'free_delivery').

discountValue.description: The value of the discount (e.g., 0.10 for 10%, 7.50 for $7.50).

minOrderAmount.description: The minimum order amount required to use this coupon.

usageLimitPerUser.description: The maximum number of times a single user can use this coupon.

totalUsageLimit.description: The total maximum number of times this coupon can be used across all users.

validFrom.description: The date and time from which the coupon is valid.

validUntil.description: The date and time until which the coupon is valid.

storeIds.description: An array of IDs of specific stores this coupon applies to. If empty, the coupon applies broadly. (Relationship: Store N:N Coupon)

productIds.description: An array of IDs of specific products this coupon applies to. If empty, the coupon applies broadly. (Relationship: Product N:N Coupon)

isActive.description: Indicates if the coupon is currently active and can be used.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the coupon record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the coupon record was last updated.

Wallet.title: Wallet
Wallet.description: Represents a user's digital wallet for storing credit or balance within the application.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Wallet entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this wallet. (Relationship: User 1:1 Wallet)

balance.description: The current monetary balance in the wallet.

currency.description: The currency in which the balance is held (e.g., 'SAR', 'USD').

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when the wallet balance was last updated.

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records all financial transactions related to user wallets, orders, or other monetary movements.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User involved in the transaction. (Relationship: User 1:N Transaction)

walletId.description: Reference to the Wallet involved in the transaction, if applicable. (Relationship: Wallet 1:N Transaction)

orderId.description: Reference to the Order associated with this transaction, if applicable. (Relationship: Order 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the transaction.

description.description: A brief description of the transaction.

paymentGatewayTransactionId.description: Optional: ID from the external payment gateway for tracking.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction was initiated.

completedAt.description: Timestamp when the transaction was completed.

Rating.title: Rating
Rating.description: Stores user ratings and reviews for various entities like stores, products, or drivers.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Rating entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who submitted the rating. (Relationship: User 1:N Rating)

entityId.description: The ID of the entity being rated (e.g., Store ID, Product ID, Driver ID).

entityType.description: The type of entity being rated ('store', 'product', 'driver').

ratingValue.description: The numerical rating given (e.g., 1 to 5 stars).

comment.description: Optional: A textual comment or review provided by the user.

orderId.description: Optional: Reference to the Order related to this rating, if it's an order-specific rating. (Relationship: Order 1:N Rating)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the rating was submitted.

Notification.title: Notification
Notification.description: Stores messages or alerts intended for users, drivers, or admins.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Notification entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User this notification is for. Null if it's a general notification. (Relationship: User 1:N Notification)

driverId.description: Reference to the Driver this notification is for. Null if it's not driver-specific. (Relationship: Driver 1:N Notification)

body.description: The main content or message of the notification.

isRead.description: Indicates whether the recipient has read the notification.

link.description: Optional: A deep link or URL that the notification directs to.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the notification was created.

Governorate.title: Governorate
Governorate.description: Defines administrative geographical regions (governorates) within the service area, often used for delivery zones.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Governorate entity.

name.description: The name of the governorate (e.g., 'Riyadh Province').

name_ar.description: The Arabic name of the governorate.

deliveryAreas.description: An array of strings or objects defining specific delivery areas/cities within this governorate.

isActive.description: Indicates if this governorate is currently serviced by the platform.

Ad.title: Ad
Ad.description: Represents an advertisement displayed within the application to users.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Ad entity.

description.description: A brief description or tagline for the ad.

imageUrl.description: URL to the image creative for the advertisement.

targetUrl.description: The URL or internal app route that the ad links to when clicked.

startDate.description: The date and time when the ad starts displaying.

endDate.description: The date and time when the ad stops displaying.

displayOrder.description: An integer to control the order in which ads are displayed.

isActive.description: Indicates if the ad is currently active and visible.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the ad record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the ad record was last updated.

Donation.title: Donation
Donation.description: Records user donations made through the platform to various causes.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Donation entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who made the donation. (Relationship: User 1:N Donation)

amount.description: The monetary amount of the donation.

causeName.description: The name of the cause or charity that received the donation.

status.description: The status of the donation transaction (e.g., 'completed', 'pending', 'failed').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the donation was made.

Subscription.title: Subscription
Subscription.description: Manages user subscriptions for premium features (e.g., VIP membership).

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subscription entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns this subscription. (Relationship: User 1:1 Subscription)

planName.description: The name of the subscription plan (e.g., 'VIP Monthly', 'Premium Annual').

price.description: The price of the subscription plan.

currency.description: The currency of the subscription price.

startDate.description: The date and time when the subscription became active.

endDate.description: The date and time when the subscription is set to expire.

status.description: The current status of the subscription (e.g., 'active', 'expired', 'cancelled', 'pending_renewal').

paymentMethod.description: The payment method used for the subscription.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the subscription record was last updated.

LoyaltyPoint.title: LoyaltyPoint
LoyaltyPoint.description: Tracks loyalty points accumulated by a user, which can be redeemed for rewards.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LoyaltyPoint entity.

userId.description: Reference to the User who owns these loyalty points. (Relationship: User 1:1 LoyaltyPoint)

points.description: The current number of loyalty points the user has.

lastUpdated.description: Timestamp when the loyalty points balance was last updated.

history.description: An array of objects detailing the history of point accrual and redemption. E.g., [{'date': '2023-10-26T10:00:00Z', 'type': 'earned', 'points': 50, 'description': 'Order #1234'}, {'date': '2023-11-01T14:30:00Z', 'type': 'redeemed', 'points': -100, 'description': 'Coupon'}]

definition.description: Top-level collection for customer profiles. `userId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid` from Firebase Authentication. Documents here are private, accessible only by the owning user.
Stores a user's digital wallet as a singleton subcollection. This enables path-based ownership with `userId` implicitly present in the path. Includes denormalized `userId`.
Stores a user's loyalty points as a singleton subcollection. This enables path-based ownership with `userId` implicitly present in the path. Includes denormalized `userId`.
Stores a user's active subscriptions. This is a subcollection of the user for path-based ownership. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence.

Stores orders placed by a specific user. This is a subcollection of the user for path-based ownership. Includes denormalized `userId` and `storeId` for authorization independence and QAPs. Access is restricted to the owning user and assigned driver/store admin.
Records financial transactions related to a specific user. This is a subcollection of the user for path-based ownership. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence.

Stores ratings submitted by a specific user. This is a subcollection of the user for path-based ownership. Includes denormalized `userId` and `entityId`/`entityType` for authorization independence and context.

Stores notifications specifically for a user. This is a subcollection of the user for path-based ownership. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence. Access is restricted to the owning user.
Records donations made by a specific user. This is a subcollection of the user for path-based ownership. Includes denormalized `userId` for authorization independence.

Top-level collection for driver profiles. `driverId` corresponds to `request.auth.uid` for driver authentication. Publicly readable (limited fields) for order assignment, private for driver's own profile management.
Top-level collection for store profiles. `storeId` can correspond to `request.auth.uid` for store admin or be a generated ID. Publicly readable for browsing, private for store admin management.

Products offered by a specific store. This is a subcollection of the store for path-based organization. Includes denormalized `storeId` for authorization independence. Publicly readable, with write access for the owning store admin.
Global collection for product and store categories. Publicly readable by all users, admin-managed.
Global collection for promotional offers. Publicly readable by all users, admin-managed.
Global collection for redeemable coupons. Publicly readable for display, admin-managed. Usage requires additional logic and rule checks.
Global collection for administrative geographical regions. Publicly readable by all users, admin-managed.
Global collection for advertisements. Publicly readable by all users, admin-managed.

Dedicated collection for managing administrative roles. The existence of a document `adminId` (matching a user's `uid`) in this collection grants administrative permissions. This enables database-backed access control (DBAC).

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure is designed with a strong emphasis on Authorization Independence and Query-as-Permission (QAP) principles, ensuring a secure, scalable, and debuggable system. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
To eliminate the need for `get()` calls in security rules and facilitate atomic operations, authorization context has been denormalized wherever a subcollection's access depends on its parent's attributes:
1.  **User-specific data:** Collections like `orders`, `transactions`, `ratings`, `notifications`, `donations`, `subscriptions`, and `loyalty_points` are nested directly under `/users/{userId}/`. This places the `userId` in the document path (e.g., `/users/{userId}/orders/{orderId}`), making it trivial for security rules to verify `request.auth.uid == userId` without retrieving the parent user document. The `Wallet` and `LoyaltyPoint` entities, being 1:1 relationships with `User`, are structured as singleton subcollections (`/users/{userId}/wallet` and `/users/{userId}/loyalty_points`) to reinforce this path-based ownership.
2.  **Store-specific products:** `Products` are nested under `/stores/{storeId}/products/{productId}`. This denormalizes `storeId` into the path, allowing rules to directly use `{storeId}` for authorization, for example, to grant a store admin write access to their products, again without needing to `get()` the parent store document.

**Query-as-Permission (QAPs):**
QAPs are achieved primarily through **Structural Segregation** and **Denormalization**:
1.  **Structural Segregation for User Data:** By placing all user-specific collections (e.g., `orders`, `transactions`) as subcollections under `/users/{userId}/`, the system inherently enforces ownership. When a user queries their orders (`db.collection('users').doc(uid).collection('orders')`), the security rules can directly match `request.auth.uid == userId` from the path. This ensures that any `list` operation on these subcollections will *only* return documents owned by the authenticated user, thus making the query itself the permission check and eliminating the need for rules to filter results after a broad read.
2.  **Structural Segregation for Store Products:** Similarly, `products` are segregated under `/stores/{storeId}/products/{productId}`. This allows for efficient and secure queries for products belonging to a *specific* store, where `storeId` is already part of the query path, making the security rules straightforward to implement (e.g., public read on all products within a `storeId`, but only store admin write).
3.  **Global, Public Collections:** Entities like `categories`, `offers`, `coupons`, `governorates`, and `ads` are placed in top-level collections. These are generally intended for broad public read access, with write access restricted to administrators. This top-level placement ensures that `list` queries on these collections are efficient and do not require complex filtering by security rules, directly supporting QAPs.
4.  **Explicit State Modeling:** Fields like `status` in `Order` and `availabilityStatus` in `Driver` explicitly define the state of an entity. This allows client-side queries to include status conditions (e.g., `where('status', '==', 'pending')`), which can then be directly and securely validated by Firestore rules, ensuring that rules do not act as filters on results but rather validate the query itself.

**DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):**
Admin roles are implemented using a dedicated top-level collection `/roles_admin/{adminId}`. The mere *existence* of a document at `roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid)` grants administrative privileges. This strategy avoids relying on custom claims, keeping all authorization data within Firestore and simplifying rule logic. Driver and Store entities also function as implicit roles, where the existence of a `driver` or `store` document matching the `request.auth.uid` grants specific operational permissions.

**Consistency and Debuggability:**
The structure employs consistent plural collection names, descriptive wildcard parameters (e.g., `{userId}`, `{orderId}`), and standardized authorization fields (`userId`, `storeId`) within documents and paths. This radical consistency enhances the clarity of intent, making security rules easier to write, understand, and debug.
